dialogue_text: I was hurt, but even then, John Carter, I did not repulse you, as I should have done, until you made it doubly worse by taunting me with having won me through combat.
source_excerpt: Then aloud she said: "Do you remember the night when you offended me? You called me your princess without having asked my hand of me, and then you boasted that you had fought for me. You did not know, and I should not have been offended;...

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: I do not need ask your forgiveness now, Dejah Thoris,
source_excerpt: "I do not need ask your forgiveness now, Dejah Thoris," I cried. "You must know that my fault was of ignorance of your Barsoomian customs. What I failed to do, through implicit belief that my petition would be presumptuous and unwelcome,...

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: You must know that my fault was of ignorance of your Barsoomian customs. What I failed to do, through implicit belief that my petition would be presumptuous and unwelcome, I do now, Dejah Thoris; I ask you to be my wife, and by all the Virginian fighting blood that flows in my veins you shall be.
source_excerpt: "I do not need ask your forgiveness now, Dejah Thoris," I cried. "You must know that my fault was of ignorance of your Barsoomian customs. What I failed to do, through implicit belief that my petition would be presumptuous and unwelcome,...

dialogue_text: No, John Carter, it is useless,
source_excerpt: "No, John Carter, it is useless," she cried, hopelessly, "I may never be yours while Sab Than lives."

speaker.notes: First-person narration suggests John Carter as the speaker.

dialogue_text: You have sealed his death warrant, my princess—Sab Than dies.
source_excerpt: "You have sealed his death warrant, my princess—Sab Than dies."
